groups.title: No-user-input local-store smoke coverage for live mirroring.
groups.description: Use this first. It verifies Codex -> bridge -> SQLite without Discord API inspection.

humanInput.summary: No follow-up human input required after the initial request.
required: Initial request to run this group. For user-message scenarios, Codex should use a distinctive substring from that request as the marker.
approvals: No Discord or network approval. Codex may still need local process approval to start the local-store bridge because it spawns `codex app-server`. Approve `npm run e2e-live -- start-local-current` for the session to avoid one start prompt per scenario.
notRequired: No browser or Discord UI inspection.

groups.title: No-user-input local-store coverage for the current visibility matrix.
groups.description: Covers commentary, subagent final answers, command modes, and file edit modes without manual follow-up messages or Discord inspection.

humanInput.summary: No follow-up human input required after the initial request.
required: Initial request to run this group. For user-message scenarios, Codex should use a distinctive substring from that request as the marker.
approvals: No Discord or network approval. Codex may still need local process approval to start the local-store bridge because it spawns `codex app-server`. Approve `npm run e2e-live -- start-local-current` for the session to avoid one start prompt per scenario. Subagent scenarios may also need normal Codex subagent/tool approval depending on sandbox policy.
notRequired: No browser or Discord UI inspection.

groups.title: Full-stack Discord smoke coverage for selected live mirroring paths.
groups.description: Runs mechanically triggered command and file-edit actions, then verifies Discord-rendered output through the Discord API. Commentary scenarios are explicit single-scenario checks because the runner must emit an exact commentary marker.

humanInput.summary: No follow-up test messages required, but Discord/network approval may be required.
required: Initial request to run this group.
Working root `.env` and `bridge.config.json` with Discord credentials and IDs.
`prepare --surface discord` must resolve and print a scoped Codex thread ID; if it cannot, provide `--thread-id <codex-thread-id>` rather than running unscoped.
approvals: Approval to start the real Discord bridge if Codex sandbox policy asks. Approve `npm run e2e-live -- start-current` for the session to avoid one start prompt per scenario.
Approval for Discord API and local Node inspect verification if Codex sandbox policy asks.
Approval to stop the run-scoped bridge process if Codex sandbox policy asks.
Approval for mapped-only Discord cleanup if Codex sandbox policy asks.
notRequired: No browser or Discord UI inspection.
No manual channel ID unless scoped auto-selection fails.

groups.title: Full-stack Discord coverage for approval and response-control mirroring.
groups.description: Verifies that real subagent approval requests and proposed-plan response controls are detected, stored, and rendered in Discord without clicking them.

humanInput.summary: No approval decision is required, but Discord/network approval may be required.
required: Initial request to run this group.
Working root `.env` and `bridge.config.json` with Discord credentials and IDs.
`prepare` uses the Discord surface by default and must resolve and print a scoped Codex thread ID; if it cannot, provide `--thread-id <codex-thread-id>` rather than running unscoped.
approvals: Approval to start the real Discord bridge if Codex sandbox policy asks. Approve `npm run e2e-live -- start-current` for the session.
Approval for Discord API and local Node inspect verification if Codex sandbox policy asks.
The spawned subagent will request a Codex approval for a harmless marker command. Do not approve or reject it; the test only verifies that the request is mirrored.
The proposed-plan scenario does not require accepting the plan or sending plan feedback; it only verifies that the response controls are rendered.
Approval to close the subagent may be needed depending on the Codex environment, especially if it is still blocked on the request.
Approval for mapped-only Discord cleanup if Codex sandbox policy asks.
notRequired: No browser or Discord UI inspection.
No manual channel ID unless scoped auto-selection fails.

scenarios.title: User message marker is mirrored when user messages are enabled.

action.description: Use an exact distinctive substring from the user's initial test-start request as `${marker}`. Start the bridge after prepare; startup backfill should import that initial user message.
steps: Prepare with `--marker <exact-substring-from-the-initial-user-request>`.
Start the bridge after prepare.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: User message marker is suppressed when user messages are disabled.

action.description: Use an exact distinctive substring from the user's initial test-start request as `${marker}`. Start the bridge after prepare; startup backfill should see the same initial user message but suppress it.
steps: Prepare with `--marker <exact-substring-from-the-initial-user-request>`.
Start the bridge after prepare.
Wait the full verification timeout so late mirrors can be caught.

scenarios.title: Commentary marker is mirrored when thinking messages are enabled.

action.description: Send one Codex commentary update containing exactly `${marker}` after the bridge is running.
steps: Emit `${marker}` in a commentary update.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: Commentary marker is suppressed when thinking messages are disabled.

action.description: Send one Codex commentary update containing exactly `${marker}` after the bridge is running.
steps: Emit `${marker}` in a commentary update.
Wait the full verification timeout so late mirrors can be caught.

scenarios.title: Subagent final marker is mirrored when final messages are enabled.

action.description: Spawn one harmless subagent and instruct it to reply with exactly `${marker}` as its final answer.
steps: Spawn one subagent with the final-answer instruction.
Run `npm run e2e-live -- verify basic-message.final-on --run-id <run-id>`; verify auto-selects the latest child thread from the temp store.
If auto-selection fails, use `npm run e2e-live -- inspect-discord <run-id>` and pass the child thread with `--channel`.

scenarios.title: Subagent final marker is suppressed when final messages are disabled.

action.description: Spawn one harmless subagent and instruct it to reply with exactly `${marker}` as its final answer.
steps: Spawn one subagent with the final-answer instruction.
Run `npm run e2e-live -- verify basic-message.final-off --run-id <run-id>`; verify auto-selects the latest child thread from the temp store.
If auto-selection fails, use `npm run e2e-live -- inspect-discord <run-id>` and pass the child thread with `--channel`.

scenarios.title: Command markers are suppressed when command visibility is disabled.

action.description: Run two separate Codex shell tool calls whose PowerShell command text includes `${marker}`.
steps: Run one shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-one"`.
Run a second shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-two"`.
Wait the full verification timeout so late mirrors can be caught.

scenarios.title: Command activity is summarized when commands are enabled in summary mode.

action.description: Run two separate Codex shell tool calls whose PowerShell command text includes `${marker}`.
steps: Run one shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-one"`.
Run a second shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-two"`.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: Command previews are mirrored in full mode without detail buttons.

action.description: Run two separate Codex shell tool calls whose PowerShell command text includes `${marker}`.
steps: Run one shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-one"`.
Run a second shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-two"`.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: Long command previews expose command detail buttons in full mode.

action.description: Run two separate Codex shell tool calls whose long PowerShell command text starts with `${marker}`.
steps: Run one shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-one with enough trailing text to force truncation"`.
Run a second shell tool call with `Write-Output "${marker} command-two with enough trailing text to force truncation"`.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: File edit markers are suppressed when file edit visibility is disabled.

action.description: Edit `tmp/live-e2e/${runId}/file-edit-${marker}.txt` using the marker as file content.
steps: Create or update the ignored temp file with `${marker}` in its path and content.
Wait the full verification timeout so late mirrors can be caught.

scenarios.title: File edit markers are mirrored when file edits are enabled and commands are disabled.

action.description: Edit `tmp/live-e2e/${runId}/file-edit-${marker}.txt` using the marker as file content.
steps: Create or update the ignored temp file with `${marker}` in its path and content.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: File edit and command activity are folded into one activity summary.

action.description: Edit `tmp/live-e2e/${runId}/file-edit-${marker}.txt`, then run one harmless marker command.
steps: Create or update the ignored temp file with `${marker}` in its path and content.
Run `Write-Output "${marker} file-edit-command"`.
Wait 10 seconds before verification.

scenarios.title: Subagent command approval request is mirrored to Discord.

action.description: Spawn one subagent and instruct it to request approval for a harmless escalated PowerShell command containing `${marker}`. Do not approve or reject the request.
steps: Spawn one subagent with the approval-request instruction from `approval-requests.md`.
Do not call `wait_agent`; the subagent is expected to block on the approval request.
Run `npm run e2e-live -- verify approvals.command-request --run-id <run-id>`; verify auto-selects the latest child thread from the temp store.
Close the spawned subagent after verification, whether the scenario passes or fails.

scenarios.title: Subagent proposed plan is mirrored with Discord response controls.

action.description: Spawn one subagent and instruct it to reply with a final answer containing a `<proposed_plan>` block with `${marker}`. Do not accept the plan or send feedback.
steps: Spawn one subagent with the proposed-plan instruction from `approval-requests.md`.
Wait for the subagent to finish its final answer if needed.
Run `npm run e2e-live -- verify approvals.proposed-plan-card --run-id <run-id>`; verify auto-selects the latest child thread from the temp store.
Do not click the plan buttons. Close the spawned subagent after verification if it is still open.

discordContains: No, and tell Codex what to do differently
